ogTitle: Swanhaven vs Swans Crossing: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Swanhaven and Swans Crossing.
heading: Swanhaven vs Swans Crossing — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Swanhaven, New South Wales and Swans Crossing, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Swans Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Swanhaven (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Swanhaven or Swans Crossing safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Swanhaven has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Swans Crossing scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Swanhaven scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Swans Crossing scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Swanhaven or Swans Crossing?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Swans Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
